name: At the border of Landes and Pyrénées-Atlantiques - loop
name_original: À la frontière des Landes et des Pyrénées-Atlantiques - boucle
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Urt traverse a landscape characterized by its proximity to the Adour River and the border region of Landes and Pyrénées-Atlantiques. The terrain features a mix of marshlands, such as the Marshes d'Orx reserve, and undulating roads with moderate elevation changes. Cyclists can expect to encounter rolling hills and scenic paths connecting historical sites and natural areas. This region offers varied road cycling experiences, from routes along wetlands to those with more significant climbs. Best road cycling routes around Urt The most popular road cycling route is At the border of Landes and Pyrénées-Atlantiques - loop, a 52.8 miles (84.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain across the regional border. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Abbaye Notre-Dame de Belloc – La Bastide-Clairence loop from Urt, a difficult 50.8 miles (81.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, passing by historical landmarks. Local road cyclists also love the Water mill – Le Petit Tourmalet loop from Urt, a 35.9 miles (57.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with a water mill feature, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Urt is defined by its marshlands, rolling hills, and proximity to the Adour River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Urt are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Urt's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Urt?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Urt, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes traverse a landscape characterized by its proximity to the Adour River and the border region of Landes and Pyrénées-Atlantiques.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Urt?
A: Road cycling routes around Urt feature a mix of marshlands, such as the Marshes d'Orx reserve, and undulating roads with moderate elevation changes. You can expect rolling hills and scenic paths connecting historical sites and natural areas, providing varied experiences from wetland rides to more significant climbs.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Urt offers routes for various ability levels. For an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40003566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dangerous 25% Descent loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated as easy and covers about 40 km with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the Urt area?
A: For a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26261358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbaye Notre-Dame de Belloc – La Bastide-Clairence loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 81.8 km path featuring significant elevation gain, passing by historical landmarks. There are 17 difficult routes in total.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Urt?
A: Yes, many routes around Urt are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964686690/at-the-border-of-landes-and-pyrenees-atlantiques-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;At the border of Landes and Pyrénées-Atlantiques - loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 84.9 km route offering varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36339209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water mill – Le Petit Tourmalet loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt;, a 57.7 km trail through varied terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Urt often pass by the Adour River and the Marshes d'Orx reserve. You can also explore historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5248143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bastide of Hastingues&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5091791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gramont Castle in Bidache&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also feature unique points of interest like a water mill.

Q: What do other cyclists think about the road cycling routes in Urt?
A: The road cycling routes in Urt are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from marshlands to rolling hills, and the scenic paths that connect natural and historical areas.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Marshes d'Orx reserve?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35768369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marshes d'Orx reserve – Orx marshes loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 120.6 km route specifically designed to take you through this significant natural area, offering unique views of the marshlands.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Urt?
A: While specific seasonal considerations for Urt are not detailed, regions with marshlands and undulating terrain like this typically offer pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can also be enjoyable, though it might be warmer.

Q: Are there any specific cycling paths or infrastructure to be aware of?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate dedicated cycling infrastructure. For instance, there's a specific &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2666167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cycle path to avoid the main road&lt;/a&gt;, which can enhance safety and enjoyment on your ride.

Q: Can I find routes that cross regional borders?
A: Absolutely. The region around Urt is characterized by its proximity to the border of Landes and Pyrénées-Atlantiques. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964686690/at-the-border-of-landes-and-pyrenees-atlantiques-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;At the border of Landes and Pyrénées-Atlantiques - loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering varied terrain as you traverse this regional boundary.
tips: An old watermill visible from the road.
Just over 1km long with maximum gradients of 8%, this short climb offers lovely views of the surrounding countryside.
A nice quiet road that avoids the sometimes quite busy Route de Bidache.
